Title: The SINA1-BSD1 Module Regulates Vegetative Growth Involving Gibberellin Biosynthesis in Tomato

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of vegetative growth and GA biosynthesis-related output; knockout causes dwarfism and reduced GA1
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hypothetical small protein with predicted C-terminal transmembrane motif
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; subcellular localization; qRT-PCR of GA biosynthesis genes; GA measurement; GA3 rescue
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BRG1 is a direct transcriptional target of BSD1 via the BBS motif in its promoter. 2. BRG1 positively regulates internode elongation and cell expansion. 3. BRG1 knockout lowers GA1 content and downregulates multiple GA biosynthesis genes, indicating an indirect positive role in GA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BSD1 regulates BRG1 transcriptionally; physical interactors not identified
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in SINA1-OX and BSD1-KO; analyzed in internodes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 application partially rescues BRG1-KO dwarfism
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression; native BRG1 promoter in reporter assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used to identify BRG1 as downregulated in both BSD1-Ri and SINA1-OX; accession NA
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function remains mechanistically unclear; broader effects unknown
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target to improve vegetative vigor and GA-associated growth, though mechanism should be clarified before deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not shown to be a transcription factor; likely acts indirectly on GA biosynthesis gene expression

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate-derived intermediate to ent-copalyl diphosphate in the GA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ent-copalyl diphosphate precursor pool/early diterpene intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with GA production; its promoter is directly activated by BSD1, supporting increased GA biosynthesis and vegetative growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; EMSA; GUS transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS2 promoter contains a BSD1-binding BBS motif. 2. BSD1 binds the CPS2 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. BSD1 activates CPS2 promoter activity, linking CPS2 expression to increased GA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Internode tissues examined for GA-related expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 treatment used to interpret GA-deficiency phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in SINA1-OX versus AC; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for increasing endogenous GA biosynthesis to enhance vegetative growth, but may risk excessive elongation if overactivated
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not manipulated genetically as a focal transgene; identified as a direct downstream GA biosynthetic target of BSD1

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early oxidation steps of GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes ent-kaurenoic acid toward GA intermediates in the GA biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with GA production; its promoter is activated by BSD1, supporting increased GA biosynthesis and growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; EMSA; GUS transactivation assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAO promoter contains a BBS motif. 2. BSD1 binds the KAO promoter in vitro. 3. BSD1 activates KAO promoter activity in transient assays, implicating KAO in the BSD1-mediated GA production pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Internode tissues examined for GA-related expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 treatment used to interpret GA-deficiency phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to enhance GA biosynthesis downstream of BSD1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In vivo ChIP-qPCR was not completed for KAO because of promoter sequence constraints around the BBS motif

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes late 3β-hydroxylation step producing bioactive GA or its immediate precursor in GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Bioactive GA or immediate precursor leading to GA1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct BSD1 target with relatively weak promoter activation; contributes to GA production and growth regulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter motif analysis; EMSA; weak GUS transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3ox4 promoter contains a BBS motif. 2. BSD1 binds its promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. BSD1 shows relatively weak activation of GA3ox4 promoter, suggesting a direct but possibly modest contribution to GA output.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Internode tissues examined for GA-related expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 treatment used to interpret GA-deficiency phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for fine-tuning bioactive GA levels, especially in combination with BSD1 modulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter activation by BSD1 was weaker than for CPS2 and KAO

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Seven in absentia (SINA) family; RING domain-containing ubiquitin ligase
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA biosynthesis via degradation of the transcriptional activator BSD1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly represses BRG1 and GA biosynthesis genes by promoting BSD1 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: May homo-/heterodimerize with other SINA proteins; functional relevance suggested but not fully resolved
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BSD1; SINA2; SINA3; possibly other SINAs via dimerization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates GA production and vegetative growth; overexpression causes dwarfism with reduced GA1 levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator upstream of BSD1 that reduces GA biosynthesis by lowering BSD1 protein abundance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; Y2H; in vitro binding; co-IP; in vitro ubiquitination assay; degradation assay in planta and cell-free extracts; metabolite profiling; GA3 rescue
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SINA1 specifically ubiquitinates BSD1 and promotes its proteasome-mediated degradation. 2. SINA1 overexpression reduces BSD1 abundance and causes dwarfism with reduced internode elongation. 3. SINA1-OX plants have decreased GA1 and are partially rescued by exogenous GA3, linking SINA1 to GA biosynthesis regulation through BSD1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss or downregulation of SINA1 could be used to maintain BSD1 abundance and support vegetative vigor, but broader pleiotropic roles of SINA proteins should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropy due to additional substrates and interactions with other SINA proteins
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MG132/MG115 proteasome inhibitors; exogenous GA3 application
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq in SINA1-OX versus AC; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SINA1/2/3 interact with BSD1 in plant cells, only SINA1 showed direct BSD1 ubiquitination activity in vitro

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant growth hormone promoting vegetative growth and internode elongation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: GA biosynthesis and vegetative growth regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BRG1; activates GA biosynthesis genes CPS2 and KAO strongly and GA3ox4 weakly
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Negatively controlled post-translationally by SINA1-mediated ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of GA production and vegetative growth; knockout causes dwarfism and lower GA1 content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive transcriptional activator connecting the SINA1 module to direct activation of GA biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Feedforward-like positive regulation of GA biosynthesis via direct activation of multiple GA pathway genes and BRG1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; RNAi background; overexpression for ChIP; Y2H; co-IP; EMSA; GUS transactivation; ChIP-qPCR; GA measurement; GA3 rescue
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BSD1 is required for normal vegetative growth and internode cell expansion. 2. BSD1 is targeted by SINA1 for ubiquitin-proteasome degradation. 3. BSD1 directly binds a novel BBS motif and activates BRG1 plus GA biosynthesis genes CPS2, KAO and GA3ox4, promoting GA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising positive regulator for increasing endogenous GA biosynthesis and plant vigor; stabilization or moderate overexpression may improve growth
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overaccumulation could alter architecture; protein stability is tightly regulated by SINA1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Internodes relevant to growth and GA assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 application; proteasome inhibitor treatments in mechanistic assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression-related assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq comparing BSD1-Ri versus AC and SINA1-OX versus AC; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recognizes a novel A/T-rich binding motif BBS: CTTATTTC/A
